title: FROM CODE TO PRODUCT: BRIDGING THE GAP BETWEEN SOFTWARE ENGINEERING AND LEAN STARTUP METHODOLOGY
author: Shokirov Abdulfayiz
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: In the contemporary digital landscape, technical proficiency alone is insufficient to ensure the success of a startup. While software engineering skills enable the creation of functional systems, the transformation of a technical solution into a sustainable digital product requires the integration of business strategy, market validation, and user-centered design. This paper examines the role of the Minimum Viable Product (MVP) as a foundational instrument in digital startup development, emphasizing its function as a learning mechanism rather than a finished product. The study analyzes how Agile development cycles and Lean Startup methodologies reduce uncertainty by enabling continuous experimentation, rapid iteration, and early customer feedback.

The study analyzes how Agile development cycles and Lean Startup methodologies reduce uncertainty by enabling continuous experimentation, rapid iteration, and early customer feedback.
